content: A spring has length ' $L$ ' and force constant ' $K$ '. It is cut into two springs of length ' $\mathrm{L}_1$ ' and ' $\mathrm{L}_2$ ' such that $\mathrm{L}_1=\mathrm{nL}_2$ ( n is an integer). The force constant of the spring of length ' $\mathrm{L}_2$ ' is
explanation:
Let the force constant (spring constant) of the original spring of length $L$ be $K$.

When a spring is cut, the force constant changes according to NCERT Textbook:

$ \text{If a spring of length } L \text{ and force constant } K \text{ is cut into two parts of lengths } L_1 \text{ and } L_2, $

then the new force constants, $K_1$ and $K_2$, of the two parts are:

$ K_1 = \frac{K L}{L_1}, \qquad K_2 = \frac{K L}{L_2} $

Given:

  • $\mathrm{L}_1 = n \mathrm{L}_2$

  • $L = L_1 + L_2 = nL_2 + L_2 = (n+1)L_2 \implies L_2 = \frac{L}{n+1}$

Calculate the force constant of piece of length $L_2$:

$ K_2 = \frac{K L}{L_2} $

Substitute $L_2$:

$ K_2 = \frac{K L}{L_2} = \frac{K L}{\frac{L}{n+1}} = K (n+1) $

Correct answer:

$ \boxed{K(n+1)} $

This matches Option A.
